title: Somalia South-Central drought — 2020-2023
brief_outcome_free: South-central Somalia (Bay region). Agropastoral livelihoods, chronic food insecurity, and a history of consecutive failed rains driving severe drought. You are the DOC drought analyst at the season's start.

peak.description: Drought-linked food/water-security crisis — see the Risk Knowledge storyline for the recorded loss & damage.

rounds.title: Seasonal outlook (T-2 seasons)
rounds.engine_state: SEAS5 forecast deficit emerging; current SPI-3 already below normal after a weak previous season

rounds.title: Deficit escalation + CDI
rounds.engine_state: Risk rising toward Assess; CDI virtual-evidence update sharpens the posterior toward High

rounds.title: Confirmation / lean season
rounds.engine_state: Sustained deficit into the lean season; DOC escalation window

evidence_cards.label: SEAS5 forecast deficit probability (vs ERA5 RP threshold)
evidence_cards.teaching_note: Slow-onset signal is STRONG and visible two seasons ahead — long lead time for anticipatory action.

evidence_cards.label: Combined Drought Indicator (JRC 14-class)
evidence_cards.source: ICPAC EADW / recompute; 14x5 noisy-likelihood applied to risk posterior
evidence_cards.teaching_note: CDI is NOT a BN parent. It updates the risk posterior multiplicatively = Pearl virtual evidence.

evidence_cards.label: Observed SPI-3 into the lean season (ERA5)

decision.checkpoint_prompt: Forecast deficit and CDI agree well ahead of the lean season. This is a STRONG, early signal. What DOC level do you set, and what anticipatory action does the long lead justify?

counterfactual.prompt: What if anticipatory action (anticipatory cash, water trucking and supplementary feeding) were triggered at T-2 seasons on the strong forecast signal?
counterfactual.narrative: Slow-onset droughts give long lead. The counterfactual shows the loss-reduction value of acting on a confident seasonal signal rather than waiting for impact confirmation.
